heading: National anthem
label: 301 National anthem
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)301(a)36:170., .Mar. 3, 1931, ch. 43646 Stat. 1508301(b)36:171., ; , ; , , .June 22, 1942, ch. 435, § 656 Stat. 380Dec. 22, 1942, ch. 806, § 656 Stat. 1077Pub. L. 94–344, § 1(18)90 Stat. 812July 7, 1976

Amendments2008—Subsec. (b)(1)(A) to (C). added subpars. (A) to (C) and struck out former subpars. (A) to (C) which read as follows:Pub. L. 110–417“(A) all present except those in uniform should stand at attention facing the flag with the right hand over the heart;“(B) men not in uniform should remove their headdress with their right hand and hold the headdress at the left shoulder, the hand being over the heart; and“(C) individuals in uniform should give the military salute at the first note of the anthem and maintain that position until the last note; and”.

Amendments2008—Subsec. (b)(1)(A) to (C). added subpars. (A) to (C) and struck out former subpars. (A) to (C) which read as follows:Pub. L. 110–417“(A) all present except those in uniform should stand at attention facing the flag with the right hand over the heart;“(B) men not in uniform should remove their headdress with their right hand and hold the headdress at the left shoulder, the hand being over the heart; and“(C) individuals in uniform should give the military salute at the first note of the anthem and maintain that position until the last note; and”.

children.heading: .—Designation
children.label: a .—Designation
children.text: The composition consisting of the words and music known as the Star-Spangled Banner is the national anthem.

children.label: A
children.text: individuals in uniform should give the military salute at the first note of the anthem and maintain that position until the last note;

children.label: B
children.text: members of the Armed Forces and veterans who are present but not in uniform may render the military salute in the manner provided for individuals in uniform; and

children.label: C
children.text: all other persons present should face the flag and stand at attention with their right hand over the heart, and men not in uniform, if applicable, should remove their headdress with their right hand and hold it at the left shoulder, the hand being over the heart; and
when the flag is not displayed, all present should face toward the music and act in the same manner they would if the flag were displayed.
